title: Use React useState for Client-Side Task State
decision: Task state will be managed entirely client-side using React's useState hook. No server-side persistence or external state libraries.
context: The to-do app needs to manage a list of tasks with add, delete, toggle, and filter operations. The data does not need to persist between page reloads for the MVP. We need to choose between client-side React state, a state management library (Redux, Zustand), or server-side persistence (database + API).
rationale: For an MVP to-do app, useState provides the simplest implementation with zero additional dependencies. The state operations (add, delete, toggle, filter) are straightforward array manipulations. Adding server persistence or external state management would increase complexity without delivering user-visible benefit at this stage.

alternatives.name: Redux or Zustand
alternatives.reason: Adds dependency and boilerplate for state that is simple enough for useState. Can migrate later if complexity grows.

alternatives.name: Server-side persistence with API routes
alternatives.reason: Not needed for MVP. Would require database setup, API routes, and error handling. Planned for REQ-6 (future: persistence).

alternatives.name: React Context API
alternatives.reason: Context is useful for sharing state across deeply nested components, but the to-do app has a flat component hierarchy. useState with prop drilling is simpler.
